summary: Backend regression on the two touched resume-download endpoints (data/content_type variable initialization). Ran full /app/backend/tests/test_admin_auth.py suite (29 tests) — all passed. Added 4 new targeted tests covering the exact review requirements: (a) auth-gating on /api/admin/applications/{id}/resume and /api/admin/inquiries/{id}/resume, and (b) 404 for non-existent (valid-ObjectId-shaped) ids. Login -> /api/admin/inbox/applications gating -> apply w/ PDF -> admin download returns application/pdf starting with %PDF verified end-to-end for both job applications and career inquiries. No regressions from the variable-initialization change.
critical_code_review_comments: admin_download_resume / admin_download_inquiry_resume use ObjectId(item_id) without try/except — if a caller passes a non-hex string (e.g. 'abc'), bson.errors.InvalidId will surface as a generic 500 instead of a clean 404/400. Consider wrapping in try/except InvalidId to return 404. This is pre-existing and unrelated to the current 2-line change, but worth hardening.

The just-added `data = None; content_type = None` initialization before the try/except is technically dead defensive code: if storage.get_object raises, the function raises HTTPException(502) and never falls through to the Response. The initialization only silences static-analysis warnings; behavior is unchanged, which was the intent — noted for reviewer awareness.

media_type fallback `rec.get('resume_content_type', content_type)` will pass content_type=None if the DB record lacks 'resume_content_type'. Starlette Response accepts None (falls back to no Content-Type header). Current records store 'resume_content_type' at upload time so this path is theoretical, but explicitly defaulting to 'application/octet-stream' would be safer.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_admin_auth.py (added 4 tests: application + inquiry resume 401 without auth, and 404 for non-existent id)
test_credentials: admin@peninsula.com.qa / Peninsula@2026 (read from /app/memory/test_credentials.md; seeded from backend/.env)
seed_data_creation: Test suite creates TEST_-prefixed insights/jobs/partners/memberships/applications/inquiries and cleans up CRUD items inside each test. Job-application and career-inquiry resume records created during resume-e2e tests are left in the DB (existing behavior — no cleanup endpoint on the API surface).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Full backend regression suite lives at /app/backend/tests/test_admin_auth.py and now has 29 tests, all green against REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. Auth flow: POST /api/auth/login -> Bearer -> /api/auth/me + all /api/admin/* endpoints. Resume flow verified end-to-end with Emergent object storage (real, not mocked): POST /api/jobs/apply (multipart with resume.pdf) -> GET /api/admin/applications/{id}/resume returns Content-Type application/pdf and body starts with %PDF. Same round-trip verified for /api/careers/inquiry -> /api/admin/inquiries/{id}/resume. Only backend was changed (2-line var init in server.py ~L441-442 and ~L471-472); no frontend or DB schema impact.
rca of the issue: N/A — verification only. The change (initializing data=None/content_type=None before the try block calling storage.get_object) is a static-analysis appeasement; runtime paths are unchanged: on storage failure the endpoint still raises 502, and on success data/content_type are overwritten before Response() is built. All 29 tests including new 404/401 assertions confirm no regression.
